value.description: A heavy brass turnstile with a rusty lever shoved into its mechanism. It looks like it might actually move now, provided you don't mind the sound of grinding metal. It has been here for as long as anyone can remember, which in at least one vendor's case is eleven minutes.
value.name: turnstile with lever

create.name: ai-turn-turnstile

value.name: turnstile with lever
value.description: A heavy brass turnstile with a rusty lever shoved into its mechanism. It looks like it might actually move now, provided you don't mind the sound of grinding metal. It has been here for as long as anyone can remember, which in at least one vendor's case is eleven minutes.

A worn stone archway marks the edge of the market. The stones are smooth from centuries of hands brushing them for luck on the way in — a tradition that predates any living vendor and which nobody has ever verified actually works. Beyond the arch, the noise hits you. A heavy brass turnstile, once immovable, now stands rotated to one side. It moves with a sound like a choir of saws. Nearby, a heap of junk has accumulated against the wall. A faded sign bolted to the archway reads TINKERMARKET in letters made from dozens of different metals.

value.name: rotated turnstile
value.description: The brass turnstile is now rotated, clearing the way north. It groans occasionally, as if offended by the sudden movement.

input.summary: Fixed the gate puzzle. The "insert lever" action now correctly leads to the turnstile being functional. I updated the turnstile to its 'rotated' state, unlocked the northern exit to Market Square, and updated the room description to reflect that the path is now clear. I also added a verb handler for 'turn' and 'push' on the turnstile to ensure future interactions work as expected.

summary: Fixed the gate puzzle. The "insert lever" action now correctly leads to the turnstile being functional. I updated the turnstile to its 'rotated' state, unlocked the northern exit to Market Square, and updated the room description to reflect that the path is now clear. I also added a verb handler for 'turn' and 'push' on the turnstile to ensure future interactions work as expected.
